beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check bike inventory for the day.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to Monday; workaholic nature means early rise to prep for the week.

beats.activity: Bike commute from Northgate to Georgetown bike shop.
beats.reasoning: Reliable bike commute on a sunny Monday; mechanic likely rides to work.

beats.activity: Repair bikes and maintain shop inventory; focus on tune-ups.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at the shop before the FIFA match; workaholic tendency means solid 2.5 hours of focused work.

beats.activity: Commute from Georgetown to downtown bar for USA vs Wales match.
beats.reasoning: Match airs at 11am; generous Seattleite values community and would catch group-stage match at a downtown bar.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match with lunch and a couple beers.
beats.reasoning: Timing aligns with 11am kickoff; social nature and generous spending make this a natural Monday outing.

beats.activity: Commute back to Georgetown bike shop.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure (9am–3pm Westlake–UW District) doesn't affect Georgetown route; returns to work.

beats.activity: Afternoon repair work, client consultations, and restocking parts.
beats.reasoning: Workaholic energy; afternoon session to make up for match time and finish the day strong.
Sunny weather perfect for evening ride home; mechanic naturally bikes commute both ways.

beats.activity: Quick stop at a local thrift store to browse vintage transit maps and home goods.
beats.reasoning: Collector of transit maps; generous with money; Monday evening thrift run fits personality and neighborhood.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner; tend to rooftop garden plots.
beats.reasoning: Urban gardening passion; evening meal prep and garden maintenance on a sunny day.

diary: Woke up early to a perfect June morning—the kind that makes you grateful you live in Seattle. Rode down to the shop in Georgetown with the sun already warm on my shoulders. The morning flew by with tune-ups and a tricky derailleur adjustment that finally clicked into place around 11. Couldn't resist ducking downtown to The Crooked Table for the USA-Wales match. The place was packed, which honestly surprised me for a Monday lunchtime, but the energy was infectious. Had a couple beers and some decent fish tacos while the game unfolded. By the time I got back to the shop, I was buzzing a little—had to focus hard on a client's consultation about upgrading their drivetrain. The afternoon drag was real, but I pushed through. Hit the thrift store on the way home because I spotted a 1970s Metro transit map in their window last week and it was finally calling my name. Ended the day tending my rooftop garden as the light softened. Those tomato plants are actually going to produce something this year.
thought_process: The World Cup match pulled me downtown, which isn't something I usually do on a work Monday, but the weather was too good to ignore and honestly the shop was slow enough that sneaking out felt justified. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I bike everywhere anyway—actually felt a little smug about that. My mood shifted between the morning grind (methodical, focused) to the buzzy social energy at the bar (loose, present) and then back into work mode, which took some recalibrating. The thrift store stop was pure impulse, but I've been thinking about that map for a week and I knew if I didn't grab it today I'd regret it. By evening I was ready for quiet—the garden felt like the right way to wind down after the beer and noise.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
